findings.message: Cesium for Unreal v2.21.1 is the LAST release supporting UE 5.4; v2.22.0+ requires UE 5.5+. Project is pinned to a legacy Cesium version from day one — recommend evaluating UE 5.5 upgrade before implementation starts.

Screenshot-diff SSIM thresholds (0.72 per-camera / 0.78 mean) are provisional; must be empirically calibrated from 3+ captures before SPEC lock. game-designer aesthetic sign-off required (Conflict C3).

Lumen screen-space temporal artifacts in PIE and first frames will cause false screenshot-diff failures unless testMode disables TAA accumulation and adds a 2-second settle period after tile-load gate.
GitHub-hosted Linux runners have no GPU; screenshot-diff (AC-V2-18) and perf gate (AC-V2-17) require self-hosted RTX 3060 runner for tag-only CI jobs.
Tile-load gate implementation is required before any screenshot-diff test can be meaningful: poll ACesium3DTileset::GetTotalNumberOfTilesCurrentlyLoading() == 0 with 30s timeout.

Recommended primary test stack: UE5 Functional Test Framework (BP-authored) for functional ACs + Python PIE screenshot-diff harness + Python perf harness + Playwright v1 suite (unchanged, separate CI lane).

20 ACs proposed (AC-V2-01..20), extending product-engineer draft AC-V2-01..12 with 8 new ACs covering: tile-load timeout, testMode determinism, throttle physics, AA gun destruction, and Windows/Linux artifact checksums.

decisions_required: OQ-01: game-designer must validate or counter the proposed SSIM thresholds (0.72 per-camera / 0.78 mean) before SPEC lock (Conflict C3).
OQ-06: Google Earth reference imagery license for private CI repo baselines — provisional approval for private use only; replace with OpenAerialMap if repo becomes public.
OQ-07: Time-of-day locked to noon (CesiumSunSky.SolarTime=12.0) in testMode — game-developer must confirm implementation feasibility.
OQ-08: Operator must register RTX 3060 machine as self-hosted GitHub Actions runner (label: gpu-rtx3060) before screenshot-diff CI is active.
D1 re-check: Given Cesium v2.21.1 ceiling, product-engineer and software-architect should evaluate UE 5.5 upgrade before SPEC is locked.
